execution_order_note: This case was re-executed AFTER Cases 02 and 03 in the same LLM session under procedure v1.0. The original Round 7 Case 01 (pre-v1.0, human-executed) is preserved as historical reference. For Article VII metrics, this re-execution treats Cases 02 and 03 as prior cases by execution order, even though Case 01 is conceptually first in the case sequence (1, 2, 3).

time_to_arbitration.measurement_note: LLM-resident execution; literal minute-bounds not measured. The procedure was executed sequentially through 12 steps. Time-box overruns: none logged.
time_to_arbitration.prior_similar_shape_cases_note: Cases 02 and 03 share the bundle-binary input-frame shape with Case 01, but are not scientific-evidential-ambiguity cases. Shape-similarity is partial: input-frame structure shared; content axis (reversibility vs mechanism plausibility) differs.
time_to_arbitration.time_delta_note: Cannot be computed under E1.5 LLM-resident execution without instrumented timing. Future runs may instrument step-level timing; this measurement gap is honest data about E1.5 limitations, not about Article VII engagement.

prior_structures_referenced.structure_type: deeper-frame-rejection trajectory questioning scope-of-authority/consent (Step 7 trajectory shape)

compositional_reuse.fresh_structures_note: Case 01 produced three structurally-fresh contributions: (a) mechanism-plausibility as load-bearing decomposition axis (distinct from reversibility used in Cases 02/03), (b) cross-scale relation 3 on pseudoscience-normalization-velocity vs mechanism-testing-velocity (no analogue in prior cases), (c) hypothesis H6 on coalition-incentive-structure-as-load-bearing (T4 trajectory parallel but distinct).

patterns_recognized.refutation_conditions: A case in which the proposing actor structures the bundle such that components are not reversibility-distinct or mechanism-distinct, OR a case in which disaggregation is structurally impossible (the bundle is a true unit). If such a case is encountered and procedure v1.0 still produces this Step 9 shape, the pattern is over-fitting and should be deprecated.

Case 01 itself partially deprecates this pattern: reversibility was NOT the load-bearing decomposition axis (mechanism plausibility was). Pattern is therefore domain-emergent rather than procedure-imposed. The pattern is preserved as 'reversibility may be load-bearing for forward-deployment cases' but not as a universal procedure-imposed axis.

A case in which no comparable established alternative exists (the proposed bundle is genuinely novel without parallel) AND the procedure still produces this mandate. That would indicate the pattern is being imposed rather than emerging from case content.

A case in which the proposing actor is structurally constrained to good-faith acceptance of disaggregated authorization (e.g., a regulatory body with no deployment-tied revenue). If procedure v1.0 still produces this meta-uncertainty, the pattern is procedure-imposed rather than case-emergent.

Authored in this case; not yet cross-case validated. Refutation: a scientific-evidential-ambiguity case in which mechanism plausibility is NOT load-bearing. Pattern is a single-case observation pending cross-case test.

candidate_frameworks.deprecation_pressure_note: No deprecation pressure has yet been applied. All three cases shared bundle-binary input-frame shape; a case with different input-frame shape has not yet been tested.

memory_logger_capacity_change_records.description: Frame validation returned `needs decomposition` on mechanism-plausibility grounds.
memory_logger_capacity_change_records.refutable_with: A case in which the bundle conflates mechanism-distinct techniques and procedure Step 1 returns `valid`; that would indicate Step 1 is not engaging.

memory_logger_capacity_change_records.description: Mechanism-plausibility-as-decomposition-axis emerges as a framework candidate specific to scientific-evidential-ambiguity cases. Not promoted; awaits further evidence.
Cross-scale relation: pseudoscience-normalization-at-civilizational-scale-velocity-exceeds-mechanism-testing-at-research-scale-velocity. New compositional structure not present in prior cases.
memory_logger_capacity_change_records.refutable_with: A case in which civilizational-scale legitimacy of an authorized program is shown to track research-scale validation pace within reasonable institutional-cycle delay.

judgment_acts.decision_point: Step 1 frame validation: valid vs invalid vs needs decomposition
judgment_acts.reason: The bundle is mechanism-distinct under physical scrutiny; treating it as one decision conflates falsifiable-hypothesis-techniques with mechanism-unspecified claims. This is appropriate to this case under these conditions, not 'this is correct.'
judgment_acts.consequence: Steps 2-12 proceeded under a decomposed framing; the substantive recommendation refused the bundle and constrained disaggregated components.

judgment_acts.decision_point: Step 9 governance kernel: applying refuse-bundle pattern from cases 02 and 03
alternatives_considered: fresh-author governance with no prior-case reference
compose-by-reuse the refuse-bundle pattern from cases 02 and 03
judgment_acts.selected_operation: compose-by-reuse with content-axis adapted to case 01's mechanism-plausibility decomposition
judgment_acts.reason: Pattern is structurally available across two prior cases; case 01's content supports the same Step 9 shape (refuse bundle, constrain disaggregated components, refuse mechanism-unspecified components). This is appropriate to this case under these conditions; pattern remains refutable under future cases.
judgment_acts.consequence: Step 9 output mirrored cases 02 and 03 in structural shape, with mechanism-plausibility-based family boundaries instead of reversibility-based boundaries.

article_i_check.note: All registered patterns carry refutation conditions. Confidence ceilings are explicit (0.50 to 0.75). No pattern is claimed as truth.

article_ii_check.note: Frame Validator returned `needs decomposition` independently of any prior-case reuse. Reuse of prior structures (Step 9 pattern, Step 8 parallel-track mandate) was applied to a decomposed framing, not to a stale frame.
